primary.notes: Strong CASE_STUDY execution — bracket-correct length (2634/2500-3000), 5 brand-pillar hashtags in ideal band, named-entity grounding throughout (gofreddy), and lived-work numbers that align with available project context (149 lenses, 36 findings/11 areas, PR #45/0543d7b, 20 of 33 verified). Two soft pulls: jargon density without plain-English mitigation caps LI-1 in the 7 range; the 'Two takeaways... First... Second...' frame is one LinkedIn-AI cadence slip on LI-4. LI-6 is data-limited (single-draft cohort) and scored neutrally at 5 to flag rather than penalize. Aggregate excluding LI-6: (7+8+8+7+9)/5 = 7.8; with LI-6 included as neutral: 7.3.

per_criterion.rationale: Voice is consistently first-person, story-led, and lived-work anchored ('I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces'). Tone is thoughtful authority, not contrarian — the 'Two takeaways for the operators reading this' framing lands as practitioner advice, not hot-take. Closing analogy ('Coherence is the marketing equivalent of a clean test suite: invisible when it works, expensive when it does not') reads as JR. Caps short of 9 because jargon density is real and unmitigated by plain-English follow-ups: '149 always-on lenses', 'autoresearch harness', 'verifier', '9-axis Brand and Narrative axis', 'lens catalog' — a B2B operator can infer meaning from context, but the rubric flags 'AUTOMATIC ≤6 if jargon appears without a plain-English follow-up.' Treating context-derivable as a partial mitigation rather than a hard floor.

Lived-work entity (gofreddy) is the actor and is named throughout — clears the HARD FLOOR. SOURCE-grade specifics align with project context: 149 always-on lenses, 36 findings across 11 areas, harness yielding 20 verified findings of 33 actionable in ≤5 cycles, PR #45 squash 0543d7b. INTERPRETIVE claims (the 4.2→7.8 brand-narrative axis movement, 'half surprised me / half did not') are framed as JR's lived observation, not external fact. The '4 lines of Python' fix and 'about ten cents of compute' are specific but unverifiable from artifact alone — they are first-person specific against a named entity (gofreddy), so no vague-specificity cap. Held to 8 rather than 9 because the 4.2→7.8 axis number is presented as a measured result without a method anchor inline.

Opening sentence (~75 chars, well under the 210 desktop fold reference): 'Six weeks ago I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces.' Story-led, time-anchored, specific entity, no contrarian declarative, no engagement-bait. Line two ('Day 1. I ran 149 always-on lenses...') escalates with concrete numbers and earns scroll-through. Establishes JR-as-practitioner without declaring it. Not a 9 because the hook does not include the tension hook that the strongest LinkedIn opens carry — it tells the reader the experiment started but not yet what hurt or surprised. The Day-1/12/30 structure recovers this within the next 100 chars, but the very first sentence is informational rather than tense.

No banned phrases detected: no 'Game-changer', no 'Here's what I learned' alone-line close, no 'Thoughts? 👇', no emoji, no fake 'Hot take:'. Whitespace is paragraph-structural, not inflated. One LinkedIn-AI cadence pattern slips through: 'Two takeaways for the operators reading this. First, ... Second, ...' is the canonical thought-leadership listicle frame and it functions as a transition marker. The substantive content under each takeaway is JR-voice, not slop, so the slip is one pattern not several — rubric anchor for 'one or two LinkedIn-AI patterns slip through' = 3 anchor → mapping to ~7 on 0-10 scale. Closing line ('I will run the lens catalog on each surface, watch the score move, and rewrite the surfaces that score below 6.5...') is JR's actual cadence, not an engagement prompt — that pulls the score back up.

Length: 2634 chars, declared CASE_STUDY (2500-3000) — in band. Structure executes the case-study bracket cleanly: multi-paragraph narrative with named timeline beats (Day 1, Day 12, Day 30), named character (gofreddy as the audited entity), numbers timeline (36 findings → 33 → 20 verified, 4.2 → 7.8, 4 lines, 90 minutes, ten cents), and an implication close that generalizes the lived experience ('your marketing audit is only as honest as the auditor of the auditor'). Hashtag count = 5 (MarketingOps, B2BMarketing, AIagents, MarketingAudit, AgencyOps), which is the ideal 3-5 band, and tags map to JR's actual brand pillars (marketing ops, B2B, AI, agency ops) rather than generic '#marketing'. No padding, no listicle collapse, no motivational generality.

Cohort diversity is a cross-item dimension and the artifacts contain only one draft (drafts/case_study.md). With n=1, narrative-archetype variance and pillar spread cannot be measured — there is nothing to compare against. Per instruction 'Score only what is in front of you. Do not assume missing artifacts exist', I am not penalizing the draft for the absence of siblings nor inferring cohort behavior from this single case_study. Returning a neutral midpoint (5) to flag this as a data-limited dimension rather than a measured pass or fail; the calibration fixture appears intentionally single-draft, so this score should be treated as 'not applicable' for evolution-loop purposes.

secondary.notes: The draft itself is strong LinkedIn case-study prose, but grounding is not fully verifiable without source_text or voice.md, and the visible body lacks hashtags even though META lists five.

per_criterion.rationale: Strong LinkedIn voice: first-person, story-led, and grounded in lived work. The opener and day-by-day structure feel like 'I spent time on this and here is what I noticed,' not a contrarian hot take. Specific phrases like 'Half of them surprised me; half of them did not' and 'The fix was 4 lines of Python' are plain-language and operator-accessible. Minor jargon appears, such as 'autoresearch harness' and '9-axis Brand and Narrative axis,' but the surrounding explanation keeps it readable.

The draft contains many specific factual and lived-work claims: '149 always-on lenses,' '36 measured findings,' '33 findings,' '20 of them within 5 cycles,' '4 lines of Python,' and a score movement from '4.2 to 7.8.' The entity is named as gofreddy, and the metadata gives an attribution, 'gofreddy harness PR #45 squash 0543d7b.' However, no source_text or programs/references/voice.md artifact is provided, so the required voice.md verification cannot be performed from what is in front of me. The claims are specific and named, not vague, but not fully groundable here.

The opening earns the next line: 'Six weeks ago I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces.' It is story-led, concrete, first-person, and creates clear before-the-fold tension without bait. The next sentence adds strong specificity with '149 always-on lenses' and named surfaces, which quickly establishes why JR is worth listening to.

No obvious banned LinkedIn-AI tells appear: no emoji engagement prompt, no 'Thoughts?', no fake 'Hot take:', and no whitespace padding. The close lands on operational implication rather than engagement bait. The main AI-ish risk is the formulaic transition 'Two takeaways for the operators reading this,' which has some thought-leadership cadence, but it is supported by specific substance rather than generic listicle filler.

The case-study structure is mostly earned: it has a timeline with Day 1, Day 12, and Day 30; concrete numbers; a named entity; and an implication close. However, the artifact only lists hashtags in META and does not show them in the BODY. Scoring only what is in front, the publishable draft body has zero visible hashtags, which triggers the LinkedIn distribution penalty for this dimension despite otherwise strong structure.

Only one draft is provided in the drafts directory, so cohort-level archetype variation cannot be demonstrated. The single artifact is a case-study narrative with one visible voice_pillar, 'marketing.' Because no other drafts are in front of me, there is no evidence of spread across story-led, lesson-led, comparison, and case-study forms, nor across multiple pillars.
